channel.title: umbralcalculations
channel.description: Visual explorations in science and computation.

item.title: Introduction
item.description: This is a collection of simulated examples of real-world actions explored through interactive dashboards.

item.title: Introduction
item.category: Simulating Real-World Systems as a Programmer
item.description: This is a collection of posts aimed at programmers and non-technical readers wanting to learn the fundamentals of simulation technology and how it can be useful to the world.

item.title: Managing antimicrobial resistance (AMR) with hospital guidelines
item.description: Antibiotic-resistant infections are rising in English hospitals: can stewardship policies bring them back down, and how much room is there to do so?

item.title: Managing flood damage risk under climate change
item.description: Climate change is making UK flooding worse: how much can natural flood management interventions offset that risk, and is more spending always better?

item.title: Energy demand response optimisation for the national grid
item.description: Grid-scale batteries promise to balance the intermittent renewables of a net-zero electricity system: but how should they be dispatched, and does the answer change as the grid gets greener?

item.title: Support policies for small business survival
item.description: UK small business support comes in different flavours (rates relief, startup grants, incubators, mentoring, etc.): how should a cash-constrained policymaker has to choose between them? To maximise what?

item.title: Evaluating rugby manager decision-making
item.description: A rugby manager picks when to substitute each position group: bring on the front-row replacements early, or save them for the closing minutes?

item.title: Simulating real-world systems
item.category: Simulating Real-World Systems as a Programmer
item.description: We started this collection of posts with a question: why simulate real-world systems at all?

item.title: Simulation architectures on different hardware
item.description: When we talk about 'classical' hardware here, we just mean standard CPUs.

item.title: Learning simulations of the real world
item.category: Simulating Real-World Systems as a Programmer
item.description: Unlike machine learning models, which typically come with standard training algorithms (like backpropagation for neural networks), simulations often need us to explicitly choose and design procedures for learning their parameters from real-world data or optimising their outputs.

item.title: Black box AI is an accelerator paradigm
item.description: Here are some opinions on the realistic potential for 'black box' AI methods to improve human prediction and decision-making processes.

item.title: Mathematical symbols vs diagrams
item.description: Mathematical concepts and tools are absolutely essential to understanding and deriving deeper relationships when analysing systems in the real world.

item.title: Why simulate real-world systems?
item.category: Simulating Real-World Systems as a Programmer
item.description: One of the most compelling uses for simulations of real-world systems is in powering decision-making technologies.

item.title: The building blocks of simulations
item.category: Simulating Real-World Systems as a Programmer
item.description: Time is probably the most important variable to track in a simulation. This is because everything that happens within a simulation only has meaning with respect to the real world if we can trace what point in simulated time that it happened.

item.title: Probabilistic thinking for simulations
item.category: Simulating Real-World Systems as a Programmer
item.description: The 'trajectory' of a simulation is the sequence of possible state values that its partitions actually take during a specific simulation run.
